items.snippet: Schadenfreude is a beast—and momentum investors are on the receiving end now. Momentum, the practice of buying the market’s best-performing stocks based on the premise they will stay the best performers, is a wonderful investing strategy when it’s working in your favor. The returns are fantastic—the Invesco S&P 500 Momentum exchange-traded fund has returned 42% a year for the past three years, more than double the SPDR S&P 500 ETF’s 20% annual return.
items.title: Review & Preview: Losing Momentum

items.snippet: Expand Energy stock has delivered a 109.0% gain over the past five years, yet the broader valuation checks still lean cheap. This raises a clear question over whether recent share price weakness has left the stock mispriced. The 109.0% return over five years points to a stock that has already rewarded patient holders, so new investors need to judge if the past run still leaves sufficient value on the table. Stronger cash generation potential from rising global natural gas demand can support...

items.snippet: STORY: United Airlines says it expects nearly $6 billion in additional fuel costs this year compared with earlier estimates. Wednesday’s warning comes after a surge in oil prices sparked by the conflict in the Middle East. The airline said the increase in prices since the beginning of July alone had added about $575 million to its expected costs for the third quarter. Shares in the company fell around 2% in extended trade following the news. Even so, United actually adjusted its full-year profit
items.title: United warns of $6 bln hit on fuel costs amid oil-price surge
